subject: civic
topic_title: Accountability Institutions: Code of Conduct Bureau and Public Complaints Commission
learning_objectives: Explain the Code of Conduct Bureau
Highlight the functions of both institutions
Understand their roles in promoting accountability

sections.heading: Code of Conduct Bureau
sections.content: The Code of Conduct Bureau is an institution established to ensure high standards of morality, accountability, and transparency in public service. It monitors the behavior of public officers and ensures compliance with ethical standards, including asset declaration.

sections.heading: Functions of Code of Conduct Bureau
sections.content: It ensures accountability in government, enforces ethical conduct among public officers, monitors asset declarations, and promotes integrity in public administration.

sections.heading: Public Complaints Commission (Ombudsman)
sections.content: The Public Complaints Commission, also known as the Ombudsman, is a government body established to investigate complaints of injustice, corruption, and maladministration by public officials or institutions.

sections.heading: Functions of Public Complaints Commission
sections.content: It investigates complaints from citizens, addresses corruption and abuse of power, protects citizens' rights, and monitors public institutions to ensure fairness and justice.
